source: iConnections Global Alts 2024 — Industry titans panel: AI, growth/crossover investing
summary: Synthetic data and video game environments will extend AI scaling laws beyond the current data ceiling
summary_extended: Baker argues that AI scaling laws will continue beyond the current data ceiling imposed by the exhaustion of human-generated text, driven by two new training data sources: synthetic data and video game environments. He contends that GPT-5 has effectively been trained on all available human-created content, creating a bottleneck that skeptics like Yann LeCun cite as evidence that scaling will plateau. Baker counters this view by noting that a human infant absorbs roughly 100x more data than GPT-5 by age 4, suggesting that vision and multimodal data represent a vast, largely untapped frontier for model training. His thesis implies that companies providing rich virtual environments or synthetic data pipelines — particularly large video game platforms with detailed 3D worlds — could become structurally important to AI development. However, Baker frames this as a forward-looking view rather than a current investment position, and the primary beneficiaries (synthetic data startups, game engine companies) are largely private or not unambiguously identifiable as US-listed pure plays.
detail: Baker argues that while GPT-5 has been trained on essentially all human-created content, synthetic data and video game environments will provide new training data sources that extend scaling laws. He notes an infant takes in 100x more data than GPT-5 by age 4, suggesting vision/multimodal data is the next frontier.
confidence_evidence: Baker presents this as his view against Yan LeCun's skepticism, uses 'I think' framing, not stated as a current investment position
quote_fragment: synthetic data is going to help scaling laws continue
tickers_implied_original: gaming companies as training data providers

ticker_audit.reasoning: Microsoft owns major gaming assets (Xbox, Activision) and Azure AI infrastructure, but the thesis does not unambiguously identify any specific public gaming company as a beneficiary — adding MSFT would be speculative overreach.
Roblox has a large virtual environment but Baker does not name or clearly imply any specific gaming platform; adding RBLX would be speculative.
